pages.raw_text:
The city council met last night


and went over some of the city
estimates, but did nothing final. 1t


will probably take more rleetings
to finish the estimates. The next
meeting is called for Saturday
night.


The $4,000 for months of this
year, and $4,000 for 1908, asked by
the street department for sprinkling
expense, is generally favored. The
chamber of commerce last night
registered recommendation of
this estimate.


1t was practically agreed to in
crease chief deputies in the treas
urer's and comptroller's Offices and
the water department, from 500
and $110 a month to $125. Council
men generally favor the appropria-
tions asked for the treasurer and
comptroller's departments.


There was a general belief yes
terday that something would be do
ing besides talk of estimates, and
in consequence r. H. Cosgrove,
manager of the fair association,
was there, evidently expecting that
the anti-poolroom repeal would be
up for further discussion.


G. B. Dennis, representing the
Warehouse Realty Co., offered on
behalf of his company to fill In the
Sprague st. trestle for s65,000 City
engineers have estimated that this
cost, if done by the city, would be
more than 100000. Mr. Dennis char
acterized the present wooden
trestle as dangerous to life.


G.. w. Boschke, chief engineer of
the O. r. & NJ was also hanging
around and gas informed that the
question of grade crossing over N.
Monroe st. was not to be taken up.
